content.verdict: Kigali Marriott is the predictable, comfortable, internationally-managed 5-star arrival/departure hotel for couples who prioritise reliability over boutique character. 254 rooms in central Kigali, multiple restaurants (Iriba grill, Cucina Italian, Sakae Japanese), full spa with hammam, executive lounge for Marriott Bonvoy elites, and a real swimming pool. Best value-luxury option in Kigali.
content.best_room: The Suite King with city view (75 m²) is the honeymoon pick — separate living room, Nespresso, executive lounge access. Standard Deluxe King rooms (35 m²) are excellent and far more affordable for a single arrival night.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival night
itinerary_7_nights.description: Land at KGL, 20 min hotel transfer. Welcome dinner at Iriba (the in-house grill — try the Rwandan beef sambaza). Early to bed for tomorrow's pre-dawn departure to Volcanoes.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Transfer to Volcanoes Park
itinerary_7_nights.description: 5am breakfast at Marriott. 2.5h drive to Kinigi for first gorilla trek the same afternoon (advanced itineraries do trek day 1) or check into Volcanoes lodge for evening briefing.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Volcanoes Park (at trek lodge)
itinerary_7_nights.description: Second gorilla trek + Dian Fossey grave hike.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Return to Kigali Marriott
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive back to Kigali (2.5h). Late lunch at Cucina (in-house Italian). Spa hammam ritual in the afternoon. Dinner at Sakae — Kigali's best Japanese.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Genocide Memorial + city + departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning at the Kigali Genocide Memorial (10 min by Uber). Lunch at Heaven Restaurant. Afternoon at the Inema Arts Center. Pool downtime. Evening departure from KGL.

caveats: International chain feel — for boutique character, choose The Retreat or Kigali Serena.
Located in central Kigali commercial district — quiet at night but not as residential as Kiyovu.
Restaurants and spa are 5-star international standard, not unique to Rwanda.
Pool is heated and substantial (25m); spa hammam is a notable feature in East African capitals.
Carbon footprint is standard hotel-chain (no offset programme like The Retreat).

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: As the safe, reliable arrival night before/after a gorilla trek — yes. As the romantic destination itself — no. Use it for predictability, comfort, and Marriott Bonvoy points.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: Year-round — Kigali altitude (1,500m) keeps temperatures mild (18–26°C). Cooler May–August dry season.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: 1–2 months for any standard room. Suite Kings and the Presidential Suite need 3+ months for peak gorilla-permit weeks.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: No — full-service hotel with families, business travellers, and conferences.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: Suite King with city view for honeymoon. Deluxe King for one-night arrival/departure transit.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Fly to Kigali (KGL). 20 min taxi/Uber to the hotel. Marriott airport transfer is $40 per car.
